summary: Iteration 2 admin CMS testing complete. Backend pytest: 29/30 passed (1 failure exposes a real bug — see below). All new endpoints (admin/stats, reviews approve/feature, contact read toggle/delete) working with proper auth guards. Frontend Playwright e2e: login (invalid->error, valid->/admin lands on Dashboard tab), Dashboard with 4 stat cards + recent messages + menu-by-category bars, Sidebar tabs (5) + sidebar badges, Menu tab category filters + delete confirm modal, Reviews tab with 4 view tabs (all/pending/approved/featured) + approve toggle + feature toggle, Homepage tab with hero/info bar/about/contact + 14 popular toggle cards + save toast, Inbox tab with 3 view tabs + read toggle + delete confirm, Logout. All data-testids in spec resolved.

critical.issue: Returns 0 reviews even though 7 exist — legacy seeded review docs lack the new `is_approved` field, so the default Mongo filter `{is_approved: True}` matches none. Public Reviews page on the website will appear empty as a regression. Fix: in seed_data, run `db.reviews.update_many({is_approved: {$exists: false}}, {$set: {is_approved: True}})` (and similar for `is_featured`); same for contacts (`is_read`). Alternatively change query to `{$or: [{is_approved: True}, {is_approved: {$exists: False}}]}`.

Counts use `count_documents({is_read: False})` / `{is_approved: False}` which exclude legacy docs missing the field. Result: Dashboard/Sidebar show inconsistent counts vs. the actual list. Observed: Dashboard card 'Inbox' says `5 / 1 unread` but Inbox tab shows `Unread 5`. Sidebar badge on Inbox shows `1` but list has 5 unread. Same root cause as above — fix legacy backfill on lifespan.

minor.issue: Returns updated review but `is_approved`/`is_featured` toggle relies on `cur.get(field, default)` — for legacy docs without the field, default is used which can produce non-intuitive first toggle (e.g., toggling `approve` on a legacy review whose stored field is missing returns `is_approved=False` first because default was True). Backfilling legacy docs would fix this too.
No brute-force lockout / rate limiting (carryover from iter1, playbook recommendation). Non-blocker for MVP.

ui_bugs.issue: Stat cards rendered correctly visually but lack `data-testid` attributes (e.g., `stat-menu`, `stat-reviews`, `stat-inbox`, `stat-homepage`). Adding them improves automated testability per project rule.
Shows `1 unread` while sibling Inbox tab shows `Unread 5` — mismatch caused by backend stats bug above. Once legacy docs are backfilled with `is_read=false`, this card will show the correct number.
Same mismatch: badge=1 but inbox unread count=5. Driven by `/api/admin/stats.unread_contacts`.

integration_issues.issue: Default query returns no reviews because legacy seeded docs lack `is_approved`. Public /reviews and homepage Featured reviews section will be empty — visible regression to end users.

issues: After logout, URL becomes /admin which redirects to /admin/login (because user=null). PRD says should land on '/'. Suggest navigate('/') after logout call.

No visible inline error toast/text was detected via standard `.error` selectors (only network 401 in console). Either ensure the error string lives in a `.error`/`role=alert` container or show a toast for failed login.

action_items: BACKFILL LEGACY DOCS on lifespan: db.reviews.update_many({is_approved: {$exists: False}}, {$set: {is_approved: True}}); db.reviews.update_many({is_featured: {$exists: False}}, {$set: {is_featured: False}}); db.contacts.update_many({is_read: {$exists: False}}, {$set: {is_read: False}}). This fixes the public reviews page (currently empty) AND the dashboard/sidebar count mismatch.
Add `data-testid` attributes to the 4 dashboard stat cards (stat-menu, stat-reviews, stat-inbox, stat-homepage) and the recent-contacts list / menu-by-category section.
Optional: redirect to '/' after admin logout to match PRD wording.
Optional: surface a visible error message (toast or .error span) on /admin/login when credentials are invalid.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py — list_reviews default filter uses `{is_approved: True}`; legacy docs missing the field are silently filtered out. Either backfill on lifespan (preferred) or change to `$or` query to handle absent field.

server.py — admin_stats `pending_reviews` / `unread_contacts` use `count_documents({field: False})` which excludes docs without the field. Same backfill fix applies. This is the ONLY reason the Inbox sidebar badge says 1 while the list shows 5.
server.py — toggle endpoints (`/reviews/{id}/approve`, `/reviews/{id}/feature`, `/contact/{id}/read`) read current value with `cur.get(field, default)`; consistent backfill removes ambiguity.
server.py is now 493 lines — well under the 700-line limit; no refactor needed yet, but consider splitting into routers (auth, menu, reviews, contact, admin) when adding more features.
Pydantic ReviewIn defaults `is_approved=True` — fine, but POST handler hard-overrides to False (correct for public submissions). Document this in OpenAPI schema if needed.
Admin /auth/login still has no rate limiting (carryover from iter1).
Frontend dashboard stat cards lack data-testid attributes (project rule).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (added 12 new tests across TestContact iter2, TestReviewsApproval, TestAdminStats classes)

success_rate.backend: 97% (29/30 — the 1 failure is a real backend data bug, not a test bug)
success_rate.frontend: 95% (all flows work; HIGH-priority data inconsistency between stats endpoint and list endpoints surfaces as wrong sidebar/card counts; logout redirect is minor UX deviation)

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing seeded 13 menu items (+ 1 lingering TEST item from iter1 = 14 visible), 6 seeded reviews (+ 1 lingering TEST_PWUser = 7), 5 lingering TEST contact messages from iter1. Pytest tests clean up their own TEST_-prefixed data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent applies the legacy-doc backfill in seed_data, re-run pytest (TestReviews::test_list_reviews should pass) and verify (a) public /reviews page lists the seeded reviews, (b) Dashboard 'Reviews' card shows pending=0, (c) Inbox sidebar badge matches the Unread tab count. data-testids used in the admin: admin-tab-{dashboard|menu|reviews|homepage|contacts}, admin-reviews-view-{all|pending|approved|featured}, admin-contacts-view-{all|unread|read}, admin-review-approve-{id}, admin-review-feature-{id}, admin-contact-toggle-{id}, admin-popular-toggle-{id}, admin-menu-confirm-delete, admin-contacts-confirm-delete. Confirm dialogs are shadcn AlertDialog — Cancel button has text 'Cancel' inside [role='alertdialog'].
rca of the issue: Legacy MongoDB documents (seeded BEFORE iter2 added `is_approved` to ReviewIn and `is_read` to ContactOut) do NOT contain those fields. Mongo equality filters like `{is_approved: True}` and `{is_read: False}` only match docs where the field exists with that exact value, so legacy docs are excluded from both list queries and count queries. Reproduction: (1) reset DB → run iter1 seed (no `is_approved` field on reviews) → restart server with iter2 code; (2) GET /api/reviews returns []; (3) GET /api/admin/stats returns pending_reviews=0 / unread_contacts=1 while /api/contact list has 5 unread items. Mitigation: in seed_data add three update_many calls to backfill missing fields with sensible defaults (is_approved=True for legacy reviews, is_featured=False, is_read=False for legacy contacts). Alternative: change list/count filters to `$or: [{field: value}, {field: {$exists: False}}]`.
